findings.summary: Transaction rebroadcast without idempotency can cause duplicate submissions when blockhash expires.
findings.solution: Application-level idempotency with task execution tracking.
findings.lessons: Use task ID as idempotency key and track execution state before broadcasting.

findings.summary: Rebroadcast loop without deduplication leads to duplicate position creation on timeout.
findings.solution: Track last known signature in task state; verify on-chain before retry.
findings.lessons: Check task execution metadata before broadcasting to prevent duplicate ops.

findings.summary: Solana blockhash deduplication expires after ~2 minutes. App-level tracking required for longer tasks.
findings.solution: Store execution metadata in task persistence layer; implement pre-flight checks.
findings.lessons: Blockhash deduplication is insufficient for long-running rebalance tasks.

recommendation.reason: Bug confirmed. Root cause is architectural - requires schema extension + executor modification + lifecycle changes.
recommendation.alternative_approach: Split into 5 phases as requested by owner. Phase 1: Schema (closeExecution/openExecution), Phase 2: Executor idempotency checks, Phase 3: Lifecycle pre-flight guards, Phase 4: Testing, Phase 5: Migration.

summary: BUG-003 confirmed. SolanaExecutor lacks application-level idempotency. Blockhash deduplication expires after ~2 min. Without task execution tracking, duplicate transactions can occur on retry.
root_cause_hypothesis: executeTx() rebuilds transactions with fresh blockhash on retry. Lifecycle calls executor.apply() without checking prior execution state. RebalanceTask schema lacks execution tracking fields.
gaps_found: SolanaExecutor.apply() returns ExecutionRecord but doesn't persist to task store
Lifecycle processTasks() has no pre-flight check for existing execution
new_bugs_risk: Adding closeExecution/openExecution to schema is safe
Pre-flight checks in executor are low risk
Reducing maxAttempts to 3 may impact delivery under severe congestion
